__doc__: NON-SENSITIVE config for the mechanical leak check in the refine-design-post skill. The actual BANNED TERMS (real employer/proprietary/internal names) are SENSITIVE and do NOT live in git — they are read from the gitignored root .env, key DESIGN_LEAK_TERMS (format: `Name|alias1|alias2, OtherName, ...` — entries comma/semicolon-separated, `|` separates a canonical name from aliases). This committed file holds only the parts that are safe to publish: `allow` (generic words that would false-match a term substring and are legitimately fine — the validator skips a hit equal to an allow entry) and this contract. CONSUMER: scripts/validate-design-clarity.js flags any case-insensitive whole-word match of a DESIGN_LEAK_TERMS entry in a /designs post body (warn-tier, never blocks). If DESIGN_LEAK_TERMS is unset/empty the leak check is a no-op (fail-open: an empty list loses advisory coverage, it does not ship a secret). Add a term the moment you notice one in a draft — edit .env, not this file. To wire up: add `DESIGN_LEAK_TERMS=...` to the gitignored root .env; `make validate-design-clarity` exports it for you.
